vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow

triage_decision.reason: This finding involves a standard file permission hardening for /etc/gshadow, a critical shadow file. The remediation is a simple chmod command that follows STIG patterns and is unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage. It is explicitly covered under the policy's safe_to_remediate category for file permissions.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; file_permissions; shadow_file
